recipe.title: c OV\..VeV\..LeV\..tltj, tViese fi.V\..t cVILps ca II\,

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 4 large chipping potatoes, such as Yukon Gold,

steps.instruction: sharp knife, cut the potatoes into long matchsticks.
about 3mm thick. Wash them well in a couple of changes of cold water to remove excess starch, then drain and pat dry on a clean tea towel.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 4 large chipping potatoes, such as Yukon Gold,
vegetable or corn oil for deep-trying

steps.instruction: Heat an 8cm depth of oil 1n a deep-fat fryer or other suitable deep, heavy pan to 120 l 40"C. Blanch the matchstick potatoes in manageable batches (a couple of handfuls at a time) for 2-3 minutes, then remove with a slotted spoon and drain 1n a colander.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 4 large chipping potatoes, such as Yukon Gold,
vegetable or corn oil for deep-trying
10 garlic cloves, peeled and thinly sliced

steps.instruction: Increase the temperature of the oil to l 60-180°C and re-fry the potatoes with the garlic slices in batches, moving them around in the pan, until golden and crisp. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on kitchen paper. Sprinkle with sea salt and serve immediately.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 4 large chipping potatoes, such as Yukon Gold,
vegetable or corn oil for deep-trying

steps.instruction: Alternatively, you can keep the straw potatoes warm, uncovered, on a baking tray in a low oven until ready to serve. They should stay crisp but if not you can briefly re-fry them in hot oil to crisp them up again.
